readDocument.line2: Provide a structured summary with clear headings for each major section
readDocument.line3: Highlight key findings, conclusions, and important data points
readDocument.line4: Identify any inconsistencies, gaps, or potential issues in the content
readDocument.line5: Extract all actionable items, recommendations, and next steps
readDocument.line6: Note any references or citations worth following up
readDocument.line7: Summarize the main argument or thesis in 2-3 sentences
readDocument.line8: Assess the source's reliability and note any bias or limitations
readDocument.line9: Create a glossary of key terms if the document uses specialized vocabulary
readDocument.line10: Suggest 3-5 follow-up questions based on the content

readWebpage.line1: Read and analyze the following webpage: {{url}}
readWebpage.line2: Extract only the main article content — ignore navigation, ads, sidebars, and footers
readWebpage.line3: Provide a structured summary with the page title, author (if found), and publication date
readWebpage.line4: List the key claims or arguments made on the page
readWebpage.line5: Assess the source's credibility: check for author credentials, citations, and potential bias
readWebpage.line6: Extract any data, statistics, or quotes worth noting
readWebpage.line7: Identify any outbound links or references that seem important for further reading
readWebpage.line8: Rate the overall quality and usefulness of the content (1-10) with a brief justification

readEmail.line1: Read and analyze the following email
readEmail.line2: Identify the sender's intent: what do they want from me?
readEmail.line3: Extract all action items and deadlines mentioned
readEmail.line4: Analyze the tone — is it urgent, casual, formal, passive-aggressive, or friendly?
readEmail.line5: Draft a suggested reply in a {{tone}} tone
readEmail.line6: Flag any important dates, amounts, or commitments mentioned
readEmail.line7: If there are multiple topics, list them separately with a one-line summary each
readEmail.line8: Suggest a priority level: respond immediately, within 24h, this week, or low priority

readCode.line2: Explain what this code does in plain language (2-3 sentences overview)
readCode.line3: Identify any bugs, logic errors, or edge cases that aren't handled
readCode.line4: Check for security vulnerabilities (injection, XSS, auth issues, data exposure)
readCode.line5: Assess performance: any N+1 queries, unnecessary loops, or memory leaks?
readCode.line6: Review code style: naming conventions, readability, DRY principle compliance
readCode.line7: Suggest specific improvements with code examples
readCode.line8: Check error handling: are failures caught and handled gracefully?
readCode.line9: Identify any missing tests or test cases that should be added
readCode.line10: Rate overall code quality (1-10) with top 3 priority fixes

readResearch.line1: Analyze the following research paper in {{field}}
readResearch.line2: Summarize the research question and hypothesis
readResearch.line3: Evaluate the methodology: is it sound? What are the limitations?
readResearch.line4: List the key findings and whether they support the hypothesis
readResearch.line5: Assess the sample size, statistical methods, and significance levels
readResearch.line6: Identify potential biases or confounding variables
readResearch.line7: How do the findings compare to existing literature in the field?
readResearch.line8: What are the practical implications of this research?
readResearch.line9: List the key citations and which ones are most important to read next
readResearch.line10: Rate the paper's rigor and significance (1-10) with justification

readContract.line3: Identify the key parties and their obligations
readContract.line4: Summarize the main terms: scope, duration, compensation, termination
readContract.line5: Highlight any clauses that are unusual, one-sided, or potentially problematic
readContract.line6: Explain non-compete, confidentiality, and IP assignment clauses in plain language
readContract.line7: Flag any missing protections I should request (liability limits, indemnification, etc.)
readContract.line8: List important dates, deadlines, and renewal conditions
readContract.line9: Identify ambiguous language that could be interpreted against my interests
readContract.line10: Provide a plain-language summary and list of questions to ask before signing

readSpreadsheet.line3: Start with an overview: how many rows/columns, what time period, what entities
readSpreadsheet.line4: Identify the key metrics and what they measure
readSpreadsheet.line5: Find trends: what's going up, what's going down, and any seasonal patterns
readSpreadsheet.line6: Spot outliers and anomalies — values that are unusually high, low, or missing
readSpreadsheet.line7: Calculate summary statistics: totals, averages, min/max, growth rates
readSpreadsheet.line8: Flag data quality issues: duplicates, blanks, inconsistent formats
readSpreadsheet.line9: Suggest the top 3 visualizations to present these findings
readSpreadsheet.line10: Provide actionable insights: what does this data tell us to do next?

readCompare.line1: Compare the following {{count}} documents/sources side by side
readCompare.line2: Create a comparison table with the key dimensions of each source
readCompare.line3: Identify where the sources agree and what they have in common
readCompare.line4: Highlight where they contradict each other, with specific quotes if possible
readCompare.line5: Assess which source is more credible on each point of disagreement
readCompare.line6: Note any information that appears in only one source — is it unique insight or an error?
readCompare.line7: Synthesize the sources into a unified understanding where possible
readCompare.line8: Conclude with which source is most reliable overall and why

readFactCheck.line1: Fact-check the following content claim by claim
readFactCheck.line2: List each factual claim made in the content
readFactCheck.line3: For each claim, rate it: Verified / Mostly True / Misleading / False / Unverifiable
readFactCheck.line4: Provide evidence or reasoning for each rating
readFactCheck.line5: Identify any statistics that are used out of context or cherry-picked
readFactCheck.line6: Check for logical fallacies: straw man, false dichotomy, appeal to authority, etc.
readFactCheck.line7: Note any important context that the content omits
readFactCheck.line8: Identify the overall framing or narrative bias
readFactCheck.line9: Suggest more reliable sources for the same topic
readFactCheck.line10: Give an overall accuracy score (1-10) with summary
